quick_facts.label: Typical award and length
quick_facts.value: B.Tech Mechanical Engineering is commonly offered as a four-year undergraduate professional programme; the precise award title may be B.Tech or B.E., depending on the university.

quick_facts.label: What the discipline covers
quick_facts.value: Mechanical engineering applies scientific analysis and engineering tools to model, design, produce and maintain products or systems containing mechanical elements.

quick_facts.label: Illustrative first-year eligibility
quick_facts.value: For VIT’s 2026 B.Tech admissions, PCM/PCMB applicants need at least 60% aggregate in Mathematics, Physics and Chemistry and at least 50% in Mathematics; requirements differ across institutions, categories and admission routes.

quick_facts.label: Lateral-entry route
quick_facts.value: State lateral-entry schemes can admit eligible diploma/D.Voc/B.Sc. candidates into the third semester (second year) of B.Tech; the Kerala 2025–26 prospectus specifies this route and its own qualifying-mark rules.

quick_facts.label: Illustrative tuition context
quick_facts.value: For 2026–27, VIT lists Mechanical Engineering in Group B, with annual tuition ranging from INR 1,95,000 to INR 4,90,000 across fee categories, plus a one-time refundable INR 5,000 caution deposit. This is an institution-specific published fee schedule, not a national benchmark.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: Eligibility is set by the admitting institution and route rather than by one nationwide B.Tech Mechanical Engineering rule. A common school-leaver route requires Class 12/equivalent with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. For example, VIT’s published 2026 criteria for programmes open to PCM/PCMB candidates require a 60% aggregate in Mathematics, Physics and Chemistry and at least 50% in Mathematics; its reserved-category/nativity provisions differ.
bullets: Applicants should check the specific college’s subject, minimum-mark, category, domicile and document requirements before applying.

For lateral entry, a state scheme may instead use a diploma, D.Voc or B.Sc. qualification. Kerala’s 2025–26 B.Tech Lateral Entry prospectus describes admission to the third semester for qualifying diploma/D.Voc/B.Sc. candidates and notes that universities may provide bridge courses for students from diverse backgrounds.

sections.heading: Duration and study structure
paragraphs: The regular-entry programme is generally structured as four academic years. VIT describes its B.Tech Mechanical Engineering as a four-year undergraduate programme, while Anna University’s affiliated-college Mechanical Engineering curriculum is arranged across eight semesters.
bullets: Lateral-entry duration and semester placement are governed by the applicable state/university rules. Kerala’s published scheme places eligible lateral-entry entrants in the third semester rather than the first semester.

sections.heading: Curriculum
paragraphs: Mechanical Engineering curricula combine mathematics, science, engineering science, design, manufacturing and laboratory/project learning. AICTE’s revised model curriculum characterises the field as using scientific analysis, problem-solving and appropriate tools to model, design, produce and maintain mechanical products and systems. Individual universities implement their own regulations, course codes, electives and credit totals.
bullets: An illustrative first semester in Anna University’s 2025 affiliated-college curriculum includes Applied Calculus, Introduction to Mechanical Engineering, Engineering Drawing, Applied Physics, Applied Chemistry, Python programming and Makerspace.

sections.heading: Admission routes
paragraphs: Admission is decentralised: engineering colleges may use national entrance examinations, state-level counselling/entrance processes, university examinations or merit processes permitted by their rules. JEE (Main) Paper 1 is explicitly designated for B.E./B.Tech, while VIT requires VITEEE for its general-category B.Tech admissions in 2026 and selects candidates by VITEEE rank followed by online counselling.
bullets: For VITEEE 2026, VIT states that the examination is computer-based, scheduled tentatively for April 28 to May 3, 2026, and that allocation in counselling depends on rank and available programme/campus/fee-category choices. Dates and procedures should be rechecked in the current admission notice.

sections.heading: Costs
paragraphs: Fees vary sharply by institution type, state, quota, campus, scholarship category, hostel choice and later revisions. As a dated private-university example, VIT’s 2026–27 fee page places B.Tech Mechanical Engineering in Group B and lists annual tuition of INR 1,95,000, INR 3,04,000, INR 4,02,000, INR 4,45,000 or INR 4,90,000 across its stated categories, with a one-time refundable INR 5,000 caution deposit. These figures do not include a universal estimate of accommodation, meals, travel, materials or other personal expenses.
bullets: For lateral-entry programmes under state systems, fee levels may be fixed or revised by the relevant government and college category. Kerala’s B.Tech lateral-entry prospectus states that fees for government, aided, cost-sharing and private/self-financing colleges are fixed by the government from time to time.

sections.heading: Next options after graduation
paragraphs: Graduates can pursue postgraduate study, employment, management-oriented careers or entrepreneurship. VIT describes its programme as preparing students for careers in engineering, technology and management, as well as entrepreneurship. For a research/technical postgraduate route, IIT Bombay’s Mechanical Engineering department lists M.Tech pathways and, for its Teaching Assistant and Research Assistant categories, bases admission on a combination of GATE score and interview performance.
bullets: M.Tech admissions are institute- and category-specific: IIT Bombay’s published 2026 Mechanical Engineering information lists two-year Teaching Assistant and three-year Research Assistant pathways, alongside sponsored and project-linked categories. Applicants should verify current GATE, institute, sponsorship and interview rules for the cycle in which they apply.
